what_it_does: Builds a single HTML presentation view with a styled comparison matrix for Render shops, Photo studios, and The Bench, using inline row data and DNA.org.checkMatrix to render checkmark-style cells with The Bench marked as the self column.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans, IBM Plex Mono, shared piece.css, and /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
Uses absolute-positioned layout elements for the matrix, lede, legend, footnote, title, foot, and mark.
Defines three columns and five comparison rows directly in the script.
Passes {selfCol:2} so The Bench column is treated as the highlighted self column.
Legend and footnote explain the gold checkmarks as the intended column read.
standouts: The matrix is data-driven through cols and rows arrays rather than hand-authored table markup.
The Bench column is explicitly framed as the self column through both copy and renderer options.
The copy asks viewers to read the gold marks down one column as a complete narrative.
conventions: Follows the piece template structure with .view, .title, .superhead, .foot, and .mark elements.
Uses DNA spacing and type CSS variables for local styling.
Relies on the shared DNA organism runtime for matrix rendering.
Keeps the piece data inline in the page script.
concerns: Layout uses fixed absolute pixel positions, which may be brittle across viewport sizes.
The comparison data and presentation copy are hard-coded in this file.
The title says the Bench does what others don't, while one row marks both Render shops and The Bench as true.
The rendered matrix depends on the global DNA.org.checkMatrix API being present.
notes: A self-column comparison slide where inline boolean data becomes a branded gold-read matrix.
questions: Should the Render shops true value in the pricing row remain, given the title's claim about what others don't do?
What exact table structure and accessibility semantics does DNA.org.checkMatrix emit?
Is the gold-only column read intended as presentation guidance or as a reusable rule for this organism?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file's comparison-matrix presentation, with a small tension where one capability is shared by Render shops and The Bench.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make The Bench's offer legible through a comparison grid where the highlighted column can be read independently as a concise positioning statement.
novelty: The file treats a highlighted matrix column as a standalone narrative, reinforced by legend copy, footnote copy, and renderer configuration.
contribution: Contributes a compact comparison-matrix substrate: inline column and row data, self-column highlighting, legend semantics, and copy that turns a column of marks into a readable positioning statement.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML presentation piece that renders a DNA check matrix comparing Render shops, Photo studios, and The Bench with The Bench highlighted as the self column.
intention_for_embedding: This was for expressing The Bench's differentiating claims as a gold-marked comparison column that can be read as its own narrative.
